methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Boom Supersonic is a Series D supersonic and hypersonic technology company founded by Blake Scholl, Joe Wilding, and Josh Krall, currently valued at $1.5B post-money following $900M+ in total capital raised. The supersonic and hypersonic sector contains a limited peer universe: two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and one material private comparable (Hermeus), reflecting the nascent and capital-intensive nature of this technology domain.

Stage-progression valuation is the primary applicable method, given insufficient peer density to support median-based triangulation. Applying a standard 1.5x step-up multiplier to the Series D post-money valuation of $1.5B yields an implied forward value of $2.2B. This multiplier reflects typical funding-round progression and capital intensity characteristics observed in frontier aerospace and defense technology companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.9B (low) to $2.6B (high), with a median point of $2.2B. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway clarity for supersonic flight operations, customer launch timelines and pre-orders, and technology development risk—none of which are quantified in available data. The valuation is highly sensitive to progression timing and magnitude of next capital raise, as well as proof-of-concept execution risk inherent to frontier hardware development.
